verdict_rationale: 3 blocking objections found: AC-06 uses forbidden substring pattern contradicting ticket M5/M6 anchored-pattern requirement; AC-03 JSON drops the post-option scoring contract (clause d) present in ticket AC-03; Risk 5 and AC-06 use ambiguous 'degrades gracefully to current behavior' that contradicts M5's explicit 'still apply whitelist' directive. These are not cosmetic -- they would directly mislead Dev into implementing the wrong behavior.

objections.title: AC-06 JSON uses forbidden substring pattern, contradicting ticket M5/M6 anchored-pattern requirement

evidence.ticket_m5_line_73: M5 says: 'anchored artifact patterns: ticket-<TASK_ID>.md, context-<TASK_ID>.json, ... NOT substring *<task-id>*'
evidence.ticket_ac05_line_144: AC-05 in ticket correctly says: 'cycle artifacts use anchored patterns (e.g. ticket-<TASK_ID>.md, context-<TASK_ID>.json, NOT substring *<task-id>*)'
evidence.ac_json_ac06_line_74: AC-06 then clause says: 'files NOT in dev-report files_modified/files_created AND NOT matching docs/dev/*<task-id>* are classified as foreign_session_candidate' -- uses the exact substring pattern the ticket forbids
evidence.codex_finding_6_applied: BA's own codex consultation finding #6 identified this exact issue and was marked 'applied' with rationale 'Updated M5 and AC-05 to use anchored artifact patterns instead of substring matching' -- but AC-06 JSON was NOT updated
evidence.ticket_risk4_line_116: Risk 4 also uses stale substring wording: 'untracked files matching docs/dev/*<task-id>*'

objections.impact: Dev implementing AC-06 would use broad substring matching (*<task-id>*) for artifact detection, which could match sibling task files and defeat the cross-session isolation mechanism. The anchored-pattern requirement exists specifically to prevent this false-positive matching.
objections.proposed_fix: Update AC-06 JSON then clause to reference 'anchored task-id artifact whitelist enumerated in M5/AC-05' instead of 'docs/dev/*<task-id>*'. Also update Risk 4 (ticket line 116) to use anchored patterns. Both AC-05 and AC-06 should use identical artifact matching semantics.

objections.title: AC-03 JSON drops the post-option scoring contract (clause d) present in ticket AC-03

evidence.ticket_ac03_line_134: Ticket AC-03 clause (d): 'a post-option handling contract specifies: the task-id is retained, the next user selection triggers three score-update.sh calls for ba/dev/qa, value="skip" triggers none'
evidence.ac_json_ac03_lines_34_39: AC-03 JSON then clause only says: 'AskUserQuestion is NOT used for rating; an <options> XML block is present with all 6 choices (5/4/3/2/1/Skip); the block appears at the very end of /close output after session summary and verdict' -- clause (d) entirely absent
evidence.ac_json_check_block: check.present array only lists element names ['<options>', '5 stars', '4 stars', '3 stars', '2 stars', '1 star', 'Skip'] -- no mention of value= attributes, task-id retention, or score-update triggering

objections.impact: Dev could pass AC-03 by implementing an <options> block with display-only labels but no machine-readable value attributes and no post-selection scoring contract. The rating would render correctly but never record scores -- the entire scoring pipeline would silently break on the new UX. QA could also pass this because the AC JSON check block has no assertion for the scoring contract.
objections.proposed_fix: Add clause (d) to AC-03 JSON then clause: '(d) machine-readable value attributes present (value="5" through value="1" and value="skip"); task-id retained in orchestrator context; next user selection triggers three score-update.sh calls for ba/dev/qa; value="skip" triggers none; options appear in user-facing text output only (NOT in close-report file)'. Add to check.present: 'value="5"', 'value="skip"'.

objections.title: Risk 5 and AC-06 use ambiguous 'degrades gracefully' that contradicts M5's explicit whitelist-preservation directive

evidence.ticket_m5_line_73: M5 says: 'When dev-report exists but baseline_head_sha is absent: still apply the dev-report/task-artifact staging whitelist; only skip the provenance sanity check.'
evidence.ticket_ac05_line_144: AC-05 clause (e) correctly says: 'when dev-report exists but baseline_head_sha is absent, the dev-report/task-artifact whitelist is still applied (only the provenance sanity check is skipped)'
evidence.ticket_risk5_line_117: Risk 5 says: 'isolation degrades gracefully to current behavior' -- 'current behavior' means stage-everything, which is the bug M5 is fixing
evidence.ac_json_ac06_line_74: AC-06 JSON then clause says: 'baseline_head_sha absence degrades gracefully' -- no specification of what is preserved vs skipped

objections.impact: If Dev reads Risk 5 or AC-06 JSON literally, 'degrades gracefully to current behavior' means reverting to stage-everything when baseline_head_sha is absent -- which reopens the exact BULK=false bug M5 is designed to fix. The ambiguity creates a gap where dev-reports without baseline_head_sha (which exist in production) would bypass isolation entirely.
objections.proposed_fix: Replace Risk 5 line 117 with: 'Mitigation: when dev-report exists but baseline_head_sha is absent, the whitelist and foreign-session exclusion are still enforced; only the provenance sanity check is skipped. When no dev-report exists at all, behavior falls back to current stage-everything with a WARNING.' Replace AC-06 JSON 'baseline_head_sha absence degrades gracefully' with: 'when baseline_head_sha is absent but dev-report exists, the whitelist and foreign-session exclusion still apply (only provenance sanity check is skipped); when no dev-report exists, graceful fallback to current behavior with WARNING'.

observations.title: Context line 19 references /commit --bulk as the problem path, but fix only applies to normal mode

evidence.ticket_context_line_19: Context says: 'No cross-session isolation prevents one session's /commit --bulk from staging another session's uncommitted files'
evidence.ticket_m6_line_74: M6 scopes isolation only to normal mode (BULK=false), BULK=true unchanged
evidence.ticket_nongloal_line_87: Won't Have: 'BULK=true cross-session isolation (bulk mode is intentionally an end-of-session sweep...)'
evidence.ba_codex_finding_7: BA's codex raised this same point and BA rejected it citing user's decomposed requirement explicitly preserving BULK=true behavior

observations.impact: Minor documentation inconsistency. The Context description implies the problem is with /commit --bulk, but the fix explicitly excludes bulk mode. This was already considered and rejected by BA with valid user-requirement justification. Not blocking, but Context line 19 should be clarified to say 'one session's /commit (normal mode)' to avoid confusion.

pass_items.assessment: Strong. BA cites specific file paths and line numbers for all claims. Evidence is backed by actual source code Read operations with line references: score-update.sh:84-98, close.md:379-393, changelog-analyst.md:67-68,81-83,113-115. Root cause analysis correctly identifies 5 independent design gaps with specific code evidence.

Good. All 10 user requirements (R1-R10) are decomposed in requirements_decomposition table. R9 (push commits) and R10 (spec-continue) correctly classified as orchestrator actions, not dev ACs. Won't Have section properly excludes negative delta changes, score-inject.sh modifications, BULK=true isolation, and the AskUserQuestion tool itself. No scope creep detected. User's universal claims ('所有正面分数', '所有6个选项') are covered by M1 (all positive deltas) and M3 (all 6 choices).

Thorough. BA investigated all 5 issue areas with specific code evidence. Codex consultation was invoked and 4 of 7 findings were applied, 2 rejected with valid user-requirement justification, 1 logged as observation. The investigation correctly identified the AskUserQuestion 4-option display limit as root cause for hidden ratings.

Correct. All 3 affected files exist and contain the code BA claims: scripts/score-update.sh (EVENT_DELTAS at lines 84-98, verified max positive +15), commands/close.md (AskUserQuestion at lines 380-386, delta comments at 371-372), agents/changelog-analyst.md (Phase 2 candidate set at lines 81-83, dev-report enrichment at 113-115). Line numbers verified against actual source.

Mathematically correct. 1/3 scaling from current values: 15->5 (exact), 8->2.67->3 (round up), 6->2 (exact), 3->1 (exact), 2->0.67->1 (round up), 1->0.33->0 (round down). All positive events capped at +5. All negative events unchanged. BA provides exact integer values in M1 table, eliminating rounding ambiguity for Dev.

AC-03 correctly specifies: (a) AskUserQuestion removal, (b) <options> XML with all 6 choices, (c) placement after session summary. The ticket's AC-03 includes clause (d) for post-option scoring contract. The mechanism correctly addresses the user's complaint about hidden 2-star and 1-star options.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 issues: (1) AC-06 substring pattern -- same as my Issue 1, confirmed valid. (2) Baseline-absent behavior contradictory between Risk 5/AC-06 and M5 -- valid new finding, incorporated as OBJ-3. (3) AC-03 JSON drops post-option scoring contract -- valid new finding, incorporated as OBJ-2. (4) Cross-session isolation scope vs /commit --bulk -- classified as observation_only since BA already considered and rejected this with valid user-requirement justification.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Incorporated 2 new codex findings as blocking objections (OBJ-2 and OBJ-3). Upgraded my draft verdict from PASS-with-1-objection to FAIL-with-3-objections. Classified codex Issue 4 as observation_only (OBS-1) since BA already addressed this with user-requirement evidence.

summary.recommendation: BA must fix all 3 objections before Dev starts. OBJ-1: Replace AC-06 JSON substring pattern with anchored-pattern reference. OBJ-2: Add clause (d) to AC-03 JSON for post-option scoring contract. OBJ-3: Clarify 'degrades gracefully' in Risk 5 and AC-06 to explicitly preserve whitelist when baseline_head_sha is absent.
